items.situation: two friends planning a birthday surprise for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, Priya's birthday is next Friday. I want to throw her a surprise party in the common room. Are you in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely! What do you need me to handle?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Could you take care of the cake? She's obsessed with lemon, so grab a lemon one from the bakery on Oak Street.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Lemon it is. Should I keep her busy beforehand too?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Actually, that'd be perfect. Maybe take her out around six so I can set up the decorations.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Got it. I'll text you when we're heading back.

questions.stem: What kind of cake does the woman ask the man to buy?
questions.explanation: 女士明确说 Priya 特别喜欢柠檬味，让男士去 Oak Street 的面包店买一个柠檬蛋糕，所以 A 正确。B 巧克力和 C 草莓都改变了对话中的关键事实，属于 wrong_detail。D 香草蛋糕在对话中从未提及，属于貌似合理但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do before the party on Friday?

options.C: Take Priya out for a walk
options.D: Invite more guests to the party

questions.explanation: 女士让男士在六点左右把 Priya 带出去，好让自己布置场地，男士答应了，因此可推断他会带 Priya 出去，C 正确。A 错在男士是去面包店买蛋糕而非自己烤，属于过度推断。B 布置装饰是女士的任务，男士负责的是拖住寿星，属于张冠李戴。D 邀请更多客人在对话中毫无依据，属于无关的下一步。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm so bored of my playlist. It's the same three pop singers on repeat. Got anything fresh?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, you should try some indie folk. There's this band called River Stone — really mellow guitar stuff.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, folk isn't usually my thing. Is it slow?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Some of it, but their newer album is upbeat, almost like pop with banjos. I'll send you the link.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'll give it a shot. If I like it, I might check out their concert next month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They're playing downtown, actually. We could go together!

questions.stem: What kind of music does the man recommend?
questions.explanation: 男士建议她尝试独立民谣（indie folk），并举了 River Stone 这个乐队，所以 B 正确。A 流行乐正是女士听腻的类型，男士没有推荐它，属于 wrong_detail。C 电子舞曲和 D 五十年代爵士都未在对话中出现，属于貌似合理但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do first?

options.D: Listen to the album the man sends

questions.explanation: 男士说会把链接发给她，女士也说愿意试听，因此她最先会去听男士发来的专辑，D 正确。A 错在女士说的是‘如果喜欢再去看演唱会’，现在就买票属于过度推断。B 删除现有歌单在对话中没有依据。C 学班卓琴只是因为男士提到‘带班卓琴的流行乐’而衍生，属于无关的下一步。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this sweater last week, but it's too small. I'd like to return it, please.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, do you have the receipt?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right here. I actually love the design — could I get the same one in a larger size?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check... we're sold out of the large in this color, but a new shipment arrives Thursday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's not great. I need something before then for a trip this weekend.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, I can give you a full refund now, or you could order online — though shipping takes about a week.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the store?

options.A: To buy a sweater for an upcoming trip
options.B: To complain about a defective sweater
options.C: To return a sweater that doesn't fit
options.D: To ask when a new shipment arrives

questions.explanation: 女士开门见山说毛衣太小、想退货，这是她来店的主要目的，C 正确。A 把情境反过来了，她是来退货而不是买新的，属于 reversed_situation。B 说毛衣有质量问题，但她只是尺码太小，并非有瑕疵，属于 wrong_topic。D 只抓住对话中提到的到货日期这个细节，偏离主旨，属于 too_narrow。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Accept a refund for the sweater
options.C: Order the sweater from the website
options.D: Exchange it for a different design

questions.explanation: 女士这周末就要出行，而店里大号周四才到货、网购又要一周才能寄到，两条路都赶不上，因此她最可能当场接受全额退款，A 正确。B 等周四到货来不及，属于被否决的选项。C 网购运输要一周同样赶不上，也被否决。D 换一个不同的款式与她‘很喜欢这个设计’的表述矛盾，属于无关的下一步。

items.situation: two students talking about a houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You've been carrying that fern everywhere. What's wrong with it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm plant-sitting my sister's fern while she's abroad, and I think I'm killing it. The leaves keep turning brown.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Brown tips usually mean the air's too dry — not too little water.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? I assumed it was thirsty, so I've been soaking it every day.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's probably the issue. Soggy roots rot. Try misting the leaves and letting the soil dry between waterings.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Huh. I had it completely backwards.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to find a replacement plant for the man
options.B: Where the man's sister is traveling
options.C: Whether the man should buy his own fern
options.D: Why the man's plant is struggling

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕蕨类植物叶子变褐、男士照料不当展开，女士帮他分析原因，主旨是这盆植物为什么长不好，D 正确。A 找替代植物在对话中根本没提，属于 wrong_topic。B 姐姐去了哪里只是背景一笔带过，抓住细节偏离主旨，属于 too_narrow。C 是否要自己买一盆蕨与情境相反，男士是在替姐姐照看现有的植物，属于 wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do now?

options.A: Water the fern even more often
options.B: Water the fern less and mist it
options.C: Return the fern to his sister
options.D: Move the fern to a sunnier window

questions.explanation: 女士指出问题在于浇水过多导致烂根，建议改成给叶子喷雾、让土壤间隔干一干，男士也承认自己做反了，因此他会减少浇水并改用喷雾，B 正确。A 浇更多水与女士的建议正好相反，属于被否决的选项。C 把蕨还给姐姐不可能，因为她正在国外，属于过度推断。D 挪到阳光更充足的窗边对话中从未讨论，属于无关的下一步。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you picked your elective yet? I'm torn between astronomy and ceramics.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm taking intro to photography. The professor lets you use the darkroom anytime.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Nice. Is there a lot of homework?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really — mostly hands-on projects, no exams. But you have to buy your own film, which gets pricey.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, my budget's tight this term. Maybe astronomy's safer, then.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Astronomy has a night lab, though — you'd be on campus at ten p.m. twice a week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't realize that. I'll check the schedule before deciding.

questions.stem: What does the man say is a downside of the photography class?

options.A: Students must buy their own film
options.D: There is a lot of written homework

questions.explanation: 男士说摄影课本身作业不多，但缺点是要自己买胶卷，而且价格不便宜，所以 A 正确。B 与事实相反，他明确说 no exams。D 也与事实相反，他说主要是动手项目、写作作业不多，两者都属于 wrong_detail。C 暗房很拥挤在对话中没有提到，反而说可以随时使用，属于貌似合理但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Sign up for the photography class
options.D: Drop her elective for this term

questions.explanation: 女士在得知天文课有深夜实验后说要先看课表再决定，因此她下一步最可能去查天文课的时间安排，C 正确。A 报摄影课不太可能，因为她预算紧张、又要买胶卷。B 立刻报天文课与她‘先看课表’的表态矛盾，属于过度推断。D 干脆放弃选修在对话中毫无依据，属于无关的下一步。
